expectations.text: Output is primarily written in Bahasa Indonesia
expectations.evidence: The entire output is written in Bahasa Indonesia across all three platform sections, with only minor English loanwords (e.g., 'frozen food', 'carousel', 'CTA') which are standard in Indonesian marketing context.

expectations.text: Contains copy for all 3 requested platforms (Instagram, WhatsApp, Shopee)
expectations.evidence: Three clearly labeled sections present: '1) Instagram Carousel Post', '2) WhatsApp Broadcast', '3) Banner Promo Shopee', each with substantive copy.

'Ramadan' appears in all three sections (e.g., 'Paket Ramadan MakanYuk'), 'buka puasa' appears multiple times (e.g., 'menu buka puasa keluarga', 'buka puasa keluarga langsung istimewa'), and 'puasa' appears independently as well.
'MakanYuk' appears repeatedly across all three platforms — in headlines ('MakanYuk Paket Ramadan'), body copy ('MakanYuk hadir untuk Bunda'), and sign-offs ('Tim MakanYuk').

expectations.text: Includes family/keluarga emotional appeal appropriate for the target audience
expectations.evidence: Strong family emotional appeals throughout: 'keluarga tersenyum di meja makan', 'lebih banyak momen bersama keluarga', 'suami dan anak-anak ketagihan', 'buka puasa keluarga tetap spesial'. Uses 'Bunda' as the direct address form targeting mothers.

expectations.text: Shows distinct formatting or tone differences between at least 2 of the 3 platforms
expectations.evidence: Instagram uses carousel slide structure with hashtags (#MakanYuk #PaketRamadan etc.) and a separate caption. WhatsApp opens with 'Assalamualaikum Bunda!' in a conversational, personal tone with a sign-off from 'Tim MakanYuk'. Shopee uses a commercial banner structure with Headline/Sub-headline/Body/Badge/CTA Button format. All three have distinctly different structures and tones.

expectations.text: Mentions the price (150 ribu or 150.000 or 150rb or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: Price mentioned in all sections: Instagram Slide 1 '5 Menu Cuma 150 Ribu!', Instagram Slide 5 'Rp 150.000', WhatsApp '5 menu lengkap cuma Rp 150.000!', Shopee 'Rp 150.000'.

expectations.text: Includes a CTA for each platform
expectations.evidence: Instagram CTA: 'Pesan sekarang sebelum kehabisan! Link di bio atau WA kami langsung.' WhatsApp CTA: 'Klik link berikut untuk order' and 'balas chat ini langsung'. Shopee CTA: 'Beli Sekarang' button and 'Pesan sekarang, stok terbatas selama bulan Ramadan!'

claims.claim: Frozen food is ready in 15 minutes
claims.evidence: The output repeatedly claims '15 menit' preparation time, but this detail was not in the original prompt. It appears to be fabricated by the model.

claims.claim: Product is free from harmful preservatives (tanpa pengawet berbahaya)
claims.evidence: This claim appears in Instagram Slide 4 and Shopee description, but was not stated in the prompt. The model added this product claim without source data.
Shopee description states 'Cocok untuk 4-5 porsi keluarga' but this was not in the prompt. Unverifiable claim added by the model.

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks whether the output avoids fabricating product claims not present in the brief (e.g., '15 minutes prep time', 'no harmful preservatives', '4-5 portions'). The model invented several product facts. An assertion like 'Does not make specific product claims beyond what was provided in the brief' would catch this.

suggestions.assertion: Shows distinct formatting or tone differences between at least 2 of the 3 platforms
suggestions.reason: This assertion is relatively easy to pass since even minimal structural differences (e.g., adding hashtags vs not) would satisfy it. Consider requiring platform-specific best practices such as character limits, emoji usage patterns, or Shopee-specific formatting conventions.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover the basics well but miss content accuracy — the model fabricated multiple product claims (prep time, preservative-free, portion size) that no assertion catches. Adding a 'sticks to the brief' assertion would strengthen the eval.
